name: Black Quarter
description: An acute, highly fatal bacterial infection of cattle causing toxaemia and emphysematous myositis. Primarily affects young cattle (6 months – 2 years).

motion_magnitude.description: Very low – severe lameness or recumbency

clinical_signs: Severe lameness – animal unable to bear weight
Hot, painful, gas-filled swelling of hindquarters or shoulders
Characteristic crackling sound (crepitus) on pressing affected area
Rapid deterioration – death within 12–36 hours if untreated
recommendations: EMERGENCY: Contact veterinarian immediately – mortality rate very high
Early high-dose penicillin may be effective in early stages
Vaccinate herd with Clostridial vaccine annually
Do not disturb or move animal unnecessarily
Isolate to prevent contaminating pasture with spores
Do NOT open or drain swellings – spreads infection
Review vaccination programme for young stock
